activeProjects.title: Tactile Appendage Kinematic Octopus System (TAKOS)
activeProjects.description: A robot octopus, developing in affiliation with ECLAIR Robotics as Tech Lead. Responsible for firmware and electronics development of appendage test bed.

activeProjects.title: TUMBLE
activeProjects.description: A continuation of TRACE (see past projects), designing, manufacturing, and coding a robot that can navigate a ball through a maze. Essentially, a robot that can play the labyrinth game. Currently in the mechanical design and fabrication stage of the project.

activeProjects.title: Dice Tower
activeProjects.description: Trained a YOLO Model to detect and classify sides of a D6 on a dice tower. Dice tower designed using Fusion360 and fabricated on an XTool P2S laser cutter.

pastProjects.title: TRACE
pastProjects.description: Ping-pong ball balancing robot designed and coded from scratch. As Tech Lead, was responsible for early PID simulation work using Trick and later mechanical design for the motor and overhead camera mounts using Fusion360. Developed in affiliation with ECLAIR Robotics.

pastProjects.title: OneOhOne
pastProjects.description: Developed a financial literacy app that leveraged banking data from Nessie API and and responses from Gemini API to help high school consumers make informed purchasing and saving decisions. Responsible for testing and verification of purchase history analysis function.

pastProjects.title: Wearable Sunlight Tracker - SASE Roots 2024 Third Place
pastProjects.description: Fabricated a wearable device using Arduino that tracks duration of time outside to encourage fitness and well being. Responsible for device firmware and Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) communication between device and phone app.

pastProjects.title: Tetrify (Accessible Tetris) - HackTX 2024 Winner!
pastProjects.description: Made in less than 25 hours for HackTX 2024 held at the University of Texas at Austin. Colaborated with three other programmers to improve accessibility in Tetris. Used Python, Mediapipe, and OpenCV to bind large arm motions to player actions to enable those with limited finger dexterity to play Tetris. Intend to extrapolate to other games and potentially everyday use in web browsers. Won Best Novice Hack.

pastProjects.title: Nest Quest - EGaDS Game Jam 2024 Winner!
pastProjects.description: Made in less than 50 hours for the 2024 EGaDS Austin Texas Game Jam. Programmed a specialized 2-Dimensional physics engine using Lua and LOVE2D that handled object acceleration and collisions to create a challenging but fair movement system. Designed a progressively challenging level system involving 4 levels to challenge players of all skills levels. Won Runner Up People's Choice Award.
